dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So did you get a chance to feel the wind and sea using the Ectoplasmic Draught? I wasn't sure how long it was going to last, and wanted you to make the most of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: That was the greatest night of my existence, by a lot. Feeling the wind and sea was the perfect end to it! At first I thought the cold was making me numb, but it was just the Draught wearing off.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That night was special for me, too. Apparently my grandmother Demeter was with a mortal once... and had my mother after that, who then had me! So I'm not a true goddess, and always had an interest in my mortal roots.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You are a true goddess, Meli. If you've a bit of mortal blood, so much the better. Anyway, that was an amazing time... but now that it's over, at least I have the memory!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We could make more sometime, perhaps. Memories that is. Because considering the Ectoplasmic Draught worked like it did... I may look into replicating it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: <Gasp> Well if my heart were beating still it likely would have burst! I'd give anything to be with you again... but unlike last time, I'll be patient about it. I swear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So did you get a chance to feel the wind and sea using the Ectoplasmic Draught? I wasn't sure how long it was going to last, and wanted you to make the most of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: That was the greatest night of my existence. It was so wonderful to feel alive with you, and then the wind and the sea! At first I thought the cold was making me numb, but it was just the Draught wearing off.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad. I was concerned I may have hurt you once again. What we have... may not be everything you want. But it means a lot to me, and it's all I can give.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It means a lot to me, too. And you've given me more than I ever could have dreamed... so how about a nice Hammer as a means of making it up to you, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You must have seen Charybdis out there, massive whirlpool-nightmare-octopus-type thing? I've seen it swallow ships like this. I don't fly anywhere close!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not so fortunate to have that option, and some nights, I've little choice but to confront Charybdis face-to... probably a face? Could use your aid if you decide to take the risk.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, absolutely not. I said I'd follow you into the sun, Meli, but Charybdis, no. My fear of flying was one thing, my fear of octopuses is another.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Something happened since the last time you were here... these ships, one after another they all sank! Never seen these waters so calm. Did you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Did I reach Chronos in my father's House and slay him? Yes. And, did he then reconstitute himself, perhaps entirely negating whatever I achieved? Also, yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I always knew you'd get him, Meli. Well as you can see, his risen fleet has risen once again! Though, sounds to me like you've found one of its weaknesses!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you've erm, uh... grown! A bit... I mean... I'm not imagining it, am I, hey? Since normally I don't quite have to crane my neck this much to look at you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Just a bit of magick, courtesy of Circe the Enchantress. A temporary boost of strength, though with what seems to be a noticeable side effect...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not a bad thing, I just couldn't help but notice...! And now I'm staring... and now, I'm going to stop. Here you go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: What happened to you, Meli, you're so small! Not that you're usually big, I mean, I mean... you've always been just right, but normally you're... just forget I spoke?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's all right, Icarus. You must be noticing the work of Circe the Enchantress, who provided one of her protective blessings for the night. Is the effect really that noticeable?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: No! No, it really isn't! Erm, I just... tend to notice the little things, I mean I notice details — of all sizes! Anyway, I like it. Not that that matters. Here! Let me get you on your way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, I want you to come back. To the Crossroads. You want to aid our efforts here, you'll do an even better job of it if you're in contact with Odysseus and all our Shades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not sure that everybody feels the same, Meli. I've been able to make do here on my own, and don't do well in crowds. Maybe from living in a prison tower all my life.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But we could see each other more? And it's really not that crowded there, is it? Just give the possibility some thought...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, all right, I will. Give it some thought, that is. But if I show up and the Witch of the Crossroads turns me into the Shade of a newt or something, it'll be on your conscience.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was so pleased to see that you decided to come back! How was it at the Crossroads after all this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: A bit uncomfortable at first, but then you showed up. And it was great to see Master Odysseus again, gave me a few flight path ideas. I didn't overstay did I...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, not at all! Well, if you didn't mind it there, then I hope you'll return. And perhaps start to think of it again as a place to stay, not just a place to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's called the Crossroads, Meli, I never considered it could be a place to stay! Hey... cheers for giving me a push.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How fares your father, by the way? You've no idea how happy I am to find Daedalus Hammers out there... I never see him around, so can never thank him in person.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh! I'll be sure to send him your regards when next I happen to run into him, though it's been a little while. I'm glad his Hammers have been of some use. Have another?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You asked about my father the other night... and truth is, though you probably already know, I never see him around either. I think he might have retired!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Daedalus, retired? I just found one of his Hammers earlier! He's been of constant help to me since all this started, he... wait... what...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Meli, I'm his apprentice! He'd been building for a long time and needed a break. I'm nowhere near him but it's not like there was anybody else.... The Hammers come in handy though?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Stop, you're hurting my head. The Daedalus Hammers I've been finding are from you? Shouldn't they be Icarus Hammers then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, we have a certain reputation to uphold. Not sure slapping my name on my father's designs would do us any good. Please... don't go telling anyone about this, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You made your own wings, munitions, apparently the latest Daedalus Hammers... you've become quite the craftsman!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Meli. My father was a master by the time he was my age, even before I died. I'll never surpass him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's not what I've said. Your work is your own, and it's good! Better than good. When are you finally going to step out from your father's shadow?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Funny question coming from you! My father may not be a god... but he's probably the greatest artist and inventor that ever lived. He'd be the first to say I'll never reach his level. Anyway, I'm through trying to prove myself to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How is it that you know so much about all the Nocturnal Arms, to have devised so many custom Hammer tune-ups you've conveniently left for me? The Arms of Night are a closely guarded secret with little room for improvement.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: My father worked for yours, remember? Came up with the interlocking chambers of the Underworld and so on? He must have earned a lot of trust... and learned a lot of secrets.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And then he shared those secrets with his apprentice who supposedly can never live up to his lofty expectations?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: If you're insinuating that I likewise earned my father's trust... fear not! When it comes to the Nocturnal Arms, I may have snooped around his notes and blueprints just a bit...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I only met my father rather recently in all of this... I thought Headmistress could be stern, but so is he. No big surprise considering the tales, but still. Perhaps your father took some inspiration from him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I almost envy you, not having known your father for too long. Once I was working with a Hammer of my father's, broke it like a dolt, called it a failed experiment. No, you're the failed experiment, he said. He was always so calm.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, that's horrid. And he clearly couldn't have meant it. You're his apprentice! Your work bears the Daedalus name.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I think my father loves me, in his way, he's just better with inventions than words. I'm sure yours is different. The god of the dead! He must be tough, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The other night, I saw these huge black wings on the horizon; definitely not a Harpy. Could have sworn it was bloody Eris... but, wasn't she still at the Crossroads, with you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, I'm not at the Crossroads either, but... unfortunately Strife Incarnate has been getting in my way. So she showed up around the time I did?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come to think of it, yes! Ah! I was beginning to worry she was here because of something I did! Never occurred to me she was just following you around, same as ever.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You shouldn't let Eris boss you around, you know. I overheard you chatting at the Crossroads. She's all talk... especially there, where her Rail of Adamant won't work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Easy for you to say, you're immortal like she is. We Shades know better than to stand up to the deathless gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't shrivel up in my presence. You're not a coward, Icarus. So why let Strife get the better of you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I figure there's already enough conflict at the moment. Eris doesn't really get to me. I just don't mind letting her think she does. Not like she's the goddess of wisdom, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been flying for a while now! At first, it brought me back to the terror I felt as a kid. Those final moments... I wanted to put myself through them, again and again. Don't know why.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, you were young and you made a mistake. What's the use torturing yourself for it for all eternity? I thought you wanted to learn to fly to prove yourself to your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Meli. I'm never going to prove myself to him. But something happened as I kept trying to use my wings... I, I started to like it! Seeing from a different point of view.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You look so in your element up there! I always believed, if I could make you whole again, perhaps some night we'd learn to fly together. I still want to feel what it's like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've no idea know how much I wanted that too. But, it was something I had to do on my own. I'd offer to take you up with me now, but... yeah, Shades and goddesses don't mix.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been flying for a while now! At first, it brought me back to the terror I felt as a kid. Those final moments... I wanted to put myself through them, again and again. Don't know why.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, you were young and you made a mistake. What's the use torturing yourself for it for all eternity? I thought you wanted to learn to fly to prove yourself to your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Meli. I'm never going to prove myself to him. But something happened as I kept trying to use my wings... I, I started to like it! Seeing from a different point of view.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You look so in your element up there! I always believed, if I could make you whole again, perhaps some night we'd fly together...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've no idea know how much I wanted that too. But, it was something I had to do on my own. I'd offer to take you up with me now, but... yeah, Shades and goddesses don't mix.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know, you've changed. You seem... more full of life. I catch these glimpses of your face while you're in flight, and... it's like there's a joy in you I never saw before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh, well... there's the flying, which admittedly doesn't get old... and then there's seeing you, taking on the legions of Chronos single-handedly. Using all your training. Wow...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't take them on single-handedly, Icarus, but... it's good to know about the flying! It must be exhilarating.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It is, Meli. You could always see right through me so no point in trying to sell it short. I hope some night you'll get a shot at it and tell me what you think.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know, you've changed. You seem... more full of life. I catch these glimpses of your face while you're in flight, and... it's like there's a joy in you I never saw before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh, well... there's the flying, which admittedly doesn't get old... and then there's seeing you, taking on the legions of Chronos single-handedly. Using all your training. Wow...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't take them on single-handedly, Icarus, but... it's good to know about the flying! It must be exhilarating.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It is, Meli. You could always see right through me so no point in trying to sell it short.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you love flying so much, why do you keep helping me? Once my father's throne is restored, the gates of the Underworld will shut, and you'll be forced to return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know. I would miss flying, sure. But, I think I could live without it if I had to — exist without it, you know what I mean. My plan is just to make the most of it, for now. We mortals are taught to believe nothing lasts forever anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I meant to tell you, Icarus, I finally got to fly! With great Selene and her moonlit steeds. I certainly see the appeal, and I didn't even get to steer the chariot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait you were all the way up there? That's amazing! The Moon herself, giving you a ride across the sky. I've thought maybe if I could just fly a bit higher, I could reach her myself, but, erm... I talked myself down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that...? No. That's really Frinos with you, isn't it? All he used to do was sit there happily! Or sadly, I could never tell. And now he's in the middle of all this...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is indeed the one and only! I never knew he had it in him either, till Headmistress let me in on more of her secrets to working with Familiars.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I always thought Familiars were just pets! Well, Frinos? It's good to see you again, mate. Keep Meli safe, will you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You should see how many inventions of mine don't work out even the slightest bit. I know some are better than others, but these are the best of the best. You have my guarantee.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I suppose it must be helpful to have someone such as me willing to test them out for you. No way of knowing if they're any good until they're put to real use, presumably?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: <Scoff> You're not testing them, Meli! My Hammers all go through extremely rigorous evaluation to earn the Daedalus name. But if you have any notes, let me know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I could have sworn I saw the hero Heracles the other night...! Launching himself from one ship to the next, crushing deathless sailors with his club! I flew well clear of him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You probably saw true. Olympus has him fighting back the Titan's legions and his fleet. We've worked together on occasion but he's kept me at arm's length...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You worked with Heracles? Oh, if only I could meet him for myself sometime...! But I don't even know what I would say. Hey! I'm like everybody else who thinks you're great!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I had a passing chance to mention you to Heracles. Unfortunately he's not keen on meeting anyone unless he's out to kill them for the gods, but... he seemed to know of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: What? How could he possibly...? He's the famous one! You sure he didn't mention Daedalus instead of me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He mentioned Daedalus, too, because... apparently he saw your final moments when you fell. I think, from what he said... he dove into the sea to find your body. To bury you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: To bury me, but I... always wondered how I got here, as my memory is murky then. But I was buried by great Heracles himself! Incredible! Tell him I, I said cheers, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: So how is the mighty Heracles lately? I'm half surprised the legions of the Titan got as far as they did knowing he's out there fighting for the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How is Heracles, that's difficult for me to say. I've never seen so fierce a warrior, but he's very guarded, too. When I told him you said cheers, he grunted; that was it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: He grunted... about me. And he knows who I am! Ah, thank you, Meli, that's amazing news! I'm sure all he needs is a little rest, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew the Nocturnal Arms could take on many different forms, but to see them like this... you must have done something to earn their favor, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I suppose? Either that or the Arms just want me to keep using them for now, in one Aspect or another. Say, how can your Hammers still work even if the Arms change shape?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I make sure of it, that's why! And seeing different Aspects put to use always gives me plenty of ideas.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You brought along the little Hammer from me, hey? First one I ever made! First that was any good, at least. Sort of a funny process, making things for making things...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, how could you give me this? You should hold onto it, as a reminder of how far you've come as a creator. I would have figured this to be one of your father's masterworks...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's much more special in your care than mine. Creating tools that others can use to reach new heights... that's how I reach them myself. You're doing me a solid here, Meli!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been getting use from that old Hammer of mine, hey? I thought maybe you'd stash it somewhere it would gather dust. It's earned a break from everything I put it through!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait you don't want me using it like this? If your intent was that it gather dust from the environment, then I think some sort of fur-lined surface would make it much more effective.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: No, no, I think the way you're using it is great. Seems like the charm on it is even stronger than it used to be! I'm just glad it's been of any help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your arm...! Never got better, did it? I'm really sorry, I should never have put you on the spot.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not your fault. I had... grown very confident in my abilities. Headmistress always said there was no way to make Shades whole again, but I thought otherwise. Overextended, learned a lesson I'm not going to forget.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I felt so bad... couldn't look at anybody in the Crossroads anymore. Thought I'd never see you again. That it'd be for the best. I... had a lot I needed to sort out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And how did it go? You learned to fly, at least! Top of your list of fears, conquered. With your father's wings, no less!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: These wings are mine, Meli. That was all part of it. Had to learn to do things for myself, like you did. And for what it's worth, I think your arm looks amazing. Now, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: How's everybody back at the Crossroads lately, hey? Master Odysseus putting all his plans to action at long last?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, yes. My route through here is thanks only to him. Say, he didn't put you up to this, did he? Deploying Shades with makeshift wings and explosives seems like something the Great Tactician would do...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm acting on my own. Although maybe we're all part of the Great Tactician's plan and just don't realize it yet. He always said we'll have to improvise... might be I finally took it to heart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fancy meeting you up here! I used to never fly anywhere near this place but noticed somewhat fewer Harpies and Automaton defenses... and, well, you. Going all right so far?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Going better than before now thanks to you. So what do you think? Does the mountain of the gods live up to everything you imagined?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Mostly, yes... considering mortals living or otherwise were never meant to see Olympus in all its glory. I'd rather see you anyway, Meli. Here, something for the rest of your climb.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not been as stormy on this mountain anymore, but is your family really going to tolerate all these Satyrs and things continuing to desecrate their home? Seems unlike them...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They're leading me to Typhon, Icarus... some scattered remnant of him beyond time. We're working with Grandfather on a cleanup effort, you could say.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Grandfather, y-you mean bloody Chronos? And, you're saying Typhon still exists? What happened to him being gone, I mean, I've seen no sign of him. This must be one of those things that's beyond mortal understanding, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't yet entirely understand it either... there's a lot we don't know about time and how it works, though we're learning, slowly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't see what's so complicated about it. Time drags on when things are bad, goes too quick when things are good. Though I'm still learning how to deal with that myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I saw huge plumes of fire shooting from the summit of Olympus recently. Thought the whole mountain was erupting! But on closer inspection, it was you! Fighting someone. And then some deranged eagle chased me off.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That was Prometheus the Titan and his pet. He's turned against the gods, and is personally leading the siege of Olympus. Claims to know the future, and to fight for mortalkind... using the flames he stole from my family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I didn't realize he'd broken free. What do mortals have to do with this whole feud with Chronos, anyhow? I thought all he wanted was his vengeance! Probably Prometheus as well...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sorry I'm late! Had a run-in with an eagle on the way. Maybe if I had a liver still I could offer it a bite and we'd become fast friends... worked for Prometheus apparently.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Beloved by birds as well as mortals. Though you don't seem to have much fondness for Prometheus yourself. Perhaps his affection for mortals isn't always reciprocated.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I mean... we've never met! When I was whole, we worshiped gods, not Titans. And Prometheus angered the gods, so... look, if you don't like him, that's enough for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Some Shades back at the Crossroads, they were going on about Prometheus lately. Some of them seem to like him, more than Chronos anyway. Must be real charming, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wouldn't say that, but he's very caught up in his cause. To let himself be tortured for the sake of mortalkind... full knowing that most mortals would never realize or care.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds a bit like a sob story to me. If he knew everything how come he let himself get caught? The gods probably would have given mortals fire anyway!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Going to introduce me to your other winged companion over there? Looks like a raven though I've never seen one quite like that before...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, right, pardon my rudeness both of you! Icarus, this is Raki, my Familiar. Raki, this is Icarus, my friend! You two are birds of a feather.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! Pleased to meet you, then, Raki. Know that I'll be studying your flight technique, since I'm always finding mine can still improve.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You said yourself you're not supposed to be here, Icarus. Chronos may have flung open the Underworld gates, but your place is still below with us. Come back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I always messed everything up, you know. I just want to be useful to someone, for once! So please... let me stay here. Let me have this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The wretches on these ships don't have their wits, but they hit hard. I, I know you're good and everything, better than good, it's just... if things go bad... what happens to you then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The first thing Headmistress taught me was how not to give in. If my strength is failing me, I use it to return to shadow whence I came. Almost a reflex at this point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: And then at the Crossroads, you're up and about by the following night, raring to go? Too bad you can't return-to-shadow back to where you left off...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I like this routine we've got going here, Meli! I feel like I could keep doing this forever. We've become ruthlessly efficient... don't even need to chitchat anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what are you even doing here? I thought you were supposed to be bound to the Underworld, but here you are, commandeering warships and whatnot!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am still bound there, I just loosened the bindings a bit. Though I feel absolutely wretched now, so if the legions of Chronos don't stop me soon, my blood-curse surely will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Really? Because you look great! Erm, you look... you look well, I mean. Unafflicted by a curse of any sort. Maybe a little seasick is all! Just... take one of these, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been some nights since we last did this hasn't it, Meli? I figured you'd show up soon enough, though... unless maybe Chronos finally got you or something terrible like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you were truly worried for me, thank you, Icarus... but also don't do that. The concern does us no good; if you're distracted, you can't fight the Titan's legions to the fullest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's all right, I get distracted all the time! Except when I'm at work, or in the sky. Which, not coincidentally, are the two things that keep me busy recently!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: There's some catastrophic storm brewing at the top of Olympus. Can't get anywhere near it. What's happening up there...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That storm conceals the real threat. Chronos awakened the Father of All Monsters and set him loose. These forces we're fighting... they're just a stalling tactic and a feint.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're saying that's bloody Typhon? Oh, gods... since I was young I'd pray to all of you for safety and good fortune from my tower. Now it's you yourselves who'd better pray...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No; we're going to destroy that thing. I don't yet know all that requires, though prayer most likely won't be necessary.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well then I'll pray just in case! Figure it couldn't hurt. Just please be careful, Meli. And before you go... take one of these.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You really went and took on Typhon, hey? I caught a glimpse of him! As close as I dared fly to that huge storm. He could have squished you like a bug! Although I guess bugs can't use magick to escape...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And bugs have wings, kind of like you! But yes, I can attest to Typhon's size and strength. Fortunately, I'm quicker... and have every god and goddess on Olympus backing me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: But their might pales in comparison to my inventions here...! Erm, I, I, I shouldn't even jest... like that. You don't suppose they heard...? Don't tell them I said anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The other night I thought I saw you launch yourself toward the Olympus summit... right into that awful storm. I couldn't even get close! What was it like up there?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh it was awful... I normally like rain, but not like that. Typhon is drawn to the Fortress of the Gods, as it's the last defense before the Palace of Zeus... likely his real goal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: So the gods keep knocking him down, but he keeps getting back up, trying over and over to accomplish the same thing? A battle of wills! A battle of wits would have been easier, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen you out here in a bit! Been busy in the Underworld, or relaxing in the hot springs with a bottle of Ambrosia or two?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I won't confess so easily. Though I suppose you're right, it has been a while since we saw each other here. Anything new to report?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd report I'm pleased to see you though that's nothing new, I guess. Your grandfather is also very good at reviving his slain followers, if that's part of your plan. It's been packed!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: By the way have you seen what we did with the springs? Good place to shed any lingering pains if you want to go?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: With you? Right now? All right! Though, don't expect the spring-water to be much good for my complexion, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Funny how utterly beyond belief life is sometimes. While I was living I could never have imagined anything like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's just a hot spring, Icarus. A purified and heavily enchanted one, but nonetheless. You've been flying over fleets of risen warships... how is this any stranger than that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh. It's mostly that I'm here with you. You're so beautiful, Meli. Not that you need us Shades and mortals to know it! Aphrodite herself must have told you, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, stop, Aphrodite has been a steadfast ally. If she was envious of me, I doubt she'd be as forthcoming with all of her most potent blessings as she has been.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: That isn't really what I meant. We mortals... tend to have a certain shame. Our bodies can be so vulnerable, and ugly, sometimes... but not gods and goddesses.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't see what's to be ashamed of. I've known Shades such as you, and Odysseus... you're close enough to gods!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah, well, that's kind of you to say. And you were right about my burdens and this place! I'm really glad we're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I only wish we could have stayed longer. Though that was great. Thank you for bringing me along.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I enjoyed our prior outing at the springs, and thought you might be open to another sometime soon. Perhaps even right now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Well, these wings, they need further maintenance but... what am I even saying. They can definitely wait. Let's go?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're quiet, Icarus. What's on your mind? Besides the further maintenance of the magnificent wings you've built.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh, it's just... most of my evenings are substantially less pleasant. I want to make this last. As well as fix the bloody wings! If they were so magnificent, they wouldn't break.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think magnificent things are often fragile things. Though they seem sturdier than you let on! You had to solve the problems that caused the famous wings of Daedalus to fail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: That failure was mine. The wings my father made were built with feathers and wax, which was all he had to work with at the time. The wax had to stay cool, but I flew off into the blazing sun. Probably should have gone out at night, in retrospect...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you didn't have the luxury of choice, since you were captives then. So what happened? The wings melted on you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, yes. Using them was such a thrill that I pushed them too hard. So I fell. These new ones use a fiber like the bones of a bird, so they don't split as easily. Though let's not talk about the past, all right? I much prefer the present.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Whew! They tell the dead to rest in peace but I've not had a break like that in far too long. Cheers, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey if you're not too busy, would you care to join me for a visit to the hot springs for a bit?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've no idea how much I want to, but I really can't right now... I told Master Odysseus I'd soon be heading out.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: I'd love a visit to the springs with you again. Shall we?

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: The springs! Not many places in the Crossroads I'd rather be.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: I'm more than happy to accompany you to the springs, Your Grace.

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...This is nice...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Could stay here all night...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Let our worries melt away...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...I needed this...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Whew, it's been a time...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Warmer than usual tonight...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...So peaceful here...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Just what I needed...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Whew, this is good...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Always pleasant...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...<Sigh> It's nice here...

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...Being here it's like I don't even have a care in the world.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...Always so peaceful here, I never want to leave, you know?

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...Beautiful night, isn't it? The way the moon shines down on everything...

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Was good to take a break and chat a bit, so cheers.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Had to call it a night sometime, but that was great.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Just what I needed... and we're both looking radiant, I'm sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, you look... the Ectoplasmic Draught. You used it, and it worked...! Can I... is it all right if I...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I was about to ask you the same thing. This is like nothing I've experienced since I was alive! And not even then! I took it all for granted then...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What makes tonight so special anyhow? I thought you were going to save the Draught for some sort of once-in-an-eternity occasion.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought about that. And then I thought about how if I could just touch you, that would make for such an occasion for me. And then I realized, I didn't want to wait. I drank the Draught once I knew you were here. It wasn't bad!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You swore you'd take this slow. What if I couldn't speak to you tonight? The Draught would have gone to waste!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're right, you're right, it's just... you're here, and... I can feel my racing heart. Meli, may I... hold you in my arms?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Choice_IcarusBecomingCloser01

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I'd like for you to do that very much. You may experience strange sensations but I'll be as gentle as I can. Please let me know if you start having discomfort of any kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: <Sigh> It's nice, the beating of your heart. And you're still very warm, you know. If not for that, I might take you for dead! You've not moved, not spoken... you all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...This must be what Elysium is like... Meli, I'm far more than all right, I'm — I'm just taking in each moment as I can. What about you? That you're still here I think is a good sign...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You may take it as such. But, oh, you're getting colder now, and... the pallor of your skin is starting to change. The Draught is losing its effect. If you hurry, you still could feel that cold wind and the spray of the sea!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I would much rather stay with you right here... but I don't wish to keep you, and I'll probably regret not taking flight like this while I still can. You're wonderful, Meli.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I just don't want to go too far, as we each have before. The side effects could be exacerbated, and... like you, I couldn't bear the thought. I think it may be best if you experienced the cold wind and the spray of the sea while you still can.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I do want to get to that. And I did get to take you by the hand. I'd stay here forever if I could but I had better make the most of this since it won't last. So bye for now, Meli.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, look what I found brewing in our cauldron over there. Care for a sip with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Another draught? Well I'll just have to drink this down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Whenever I'm alive like that again, I want to be with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fine by me...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Got something for you here... well, for the both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I want to feel alive again right now, and in your company.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You're the best there ever was or ever will be, Meli.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're not bad, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Here's another Draught for whenever you desire... perhaps even now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know I couldn't think about anything else since last time, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I only wish those draughts of yours could last forever.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They work well enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Look what I brewed up. If you're not too busy... we could go back to the tent?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You sure? I can practically feel my heart racing already...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Best night of my entire existence. Since last time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You could save this for whenever you want... though, if you're not too busy...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I really should be taking off soon... but I'm also not insane.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Each moment I can be with you is better than the last.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thought you might like to have another Ectoplasmic Draught. Perhaps with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You read my mind or stared into my soul. Whichever one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Erm, by the way... I really like what you've done to your tent.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nice, isn't it...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Was just out for a little jaunt is all and figured I'd stop in. Was good seeing you, Meli.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Rough waters this evening, though the skies above aren't bad. You take care of yourself, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Think I saw Eris further up the coast. How about you bring her a little surprise?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: What I wouldn't give to stay here longer and catch up, but I know it's not the time or place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You were amazing, Meli. I saw everything up there! Keep going, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got places to be, I know. Fortunately, I've got just the thing to help you get there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Think we'll look back someday on all of this and miss it, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know how you handle this night after night, though I am glad you do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Always good to see you, Meli, even for a bit. Nobody else will test this stuff for me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The coast is clear for now! Though, past this point, no guarantees.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Think I took care of one or two of them back there. Better than nothing, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been tinkering around with these. Take whichever one you want!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whichever one of these you choose comes with a total satisfaction guarantee.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The mountain isn't far from here. Maybe these will help you make the climb.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know that you don't have a lot of time, so here. For the road ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Still perfectly all right...! Had me a little worried for a moment there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd fly you right up to the palace of the gods right now, if only I could...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Anything you need, just say the word. Though right now, this is all I've got.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Even the gods themselves need somebody to fight for them, don't they...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're more than halfway there, Meli... or pretty close, I think. No stopping now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll have to catch up when you're free. No rush! Got all the time in the world.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Only so much I can do to help! But then it's something, hey? So here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: This ship feels like it's going to sink at any moment, so let's not stick around...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe you'll find some use in one of these. They're all I've got tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: These aren't up to my father's standard, but they're not too bad I think...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep thinking how I've never felt so alive as when I'm here with you...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Olympus isn't too much farther. I'll fly on ahead and draw some of the heat.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: A lot of Shades out there believe in you and what you're working to achieve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Can I just say, these wings hold up much better than the ones my father made?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Safe to say I never imagined my afterlife would turn out anything like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you can handle yourself, but let me see what I can do to help here. Please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Always a pleasure, Meli... I'm back on my feet but still feel like I'm flying...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: How about we try this at the Crossroads sometime, hey? Harder to concentrate here what with all the sunken ships and deathless crewmates and whatnot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's really you. Before you say anything, I know I'm not supposed to be here, but I spotted this ship and had to drop in for a closer look. And now I'm going to take off! So let's pretend this never happened, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hold it right there, Icarus! I'm not about to let you just fly out on me again without a chat at least. To start with, what are you doing in the middle of this blasted war?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The same thing I've been doing: slowing these bastards down. If everyone like me pitched in a little bit, this war would have been well over by now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That may be so. But this is a matter between gods and Titans. I wouldn't want you to get hurt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Bit late for that, Meli! Anyway, I've kept you for too long, but let me give you something I've been working on before I go — and these all work. I swear!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey if you don't have to fly off just yet, how about a visit to the fishing pier with me? It's nice and quiet over there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure, that sounds great! Though I should note I've never learned to fish, so fair warning I will not be any good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Where I was from, fish were a major source of sustenance. It's strange, I... I never really thought that they were living creatures, same as me. Well, not same as me, but you get the idea. I was a kid! What did I know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Everything you mortals eat is living, or once was. I'm glad I have no need of sustenance... I've always found it quite distasteful, the idea of having to consume lives just to prolong one's own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: We mortals are all about having to do distasteful things. That's one of the main differences between us and you, along with us being so easy to kill off. What choice do we have but to try and survive?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No choice at all, perhaps. Much in nature struggles to survive, from the flower yearning for a bit of light, to you, trying to figure out how to ask me to teach you to fish!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Haha. Well I understand it's necessary to be silent... so, why don't you provide a demonstration and I'll carefully observe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I really did enjoy that, even just the quiet-waiting part. Thanks for bringing me along.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was thinking maybe you could join me by the fishing pier again, if you don't mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: As much as I would like that, I can't stick around much longer here tonight. Some other time though, hey?

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Still don't know how to fish too well, but if you're willing to instruct...

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: If you've got the lures, I've got the patience to stand quietly.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Need a fishing partner, I'm your Shade. Now let me at 'em.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...I'd a pleasant time back there. Cheers for inviting me along.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...So peaceful back there! Long as you're not a fish, I guess.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...We must have scared most of them off. Was that the goal?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You can fly wherever you want now! But, I'm glad you didn't go so far I couldn't catch up. Here! In light of your godlike wings, a little gift.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh come on, just because I've been flying around near Olympus, doesn't mean I get to feast like I'm from there! Maybe with your permission, hey? Know what, I got something for you, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The last Nectar you got me was so good, can't even imagine what it would have been like while I was still alive. Wasn't going to ask for another, but... since you're offering!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm offering, so here! Maybe it's even better now that you're a Shade. You mortals had to eat and drink out of necessity. But Nectar helps fulfill a different kind of need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: As a mortal kid confined to a tower, I was relatively well provided for, but this? I know better than to envy the gods, especially with what they're going through lately... but they definitely know their drink.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The gods have always lived well... but your own life was so short and difficult. You must still have a lot of making up to do, and I thought maybe this could help!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Way back when, I always wanted to find you the best bottle of Nectar I could... but I never did. Adding to the list of things I perhaps should have approached differently.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please don't dwell on the past so much. You thought you'd never see me again, but I'm here, now. I appreciate this moment that we have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Can hardly feel this bottle... just its weight. Though I still have fond memories to fill the gaps. It's funny, I've lost track of how long I've been dead, but still not used to it entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps it's impossible to become entirely accustomed to the way we are. Though, I'm surprised you have any nostalgia for a time when you were young.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're one to talk! How many times have you reminisced about your quiet evenings listening to the river and the frogs? Sometimes we forget the pain, and sometimes it's all we remember. Though I'll remember this too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I was so reckless when I was a kid. My father always told me to be careful, but I was stuck in a bloody tower... couldn't do anything to hurt myself if I tried. But now... I just don't want to mess things up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For someone who spends so much time up in the clouds, you're far too stuck in your own head at times. This isn't a test, Icarus. It's me. And I simply like it when you're you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Meli, look at us...! You're the immortal Princess of the Underworld; I'm a Shade of a failed apprentice who couldn't follow one simple instruction from his father and got dead ahead of schedule. This is what you like?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, because that isn't who you are. I have no pity for you; why do you have so much for yourself? Cast it aside. But your true feelings, you should never hide from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll try... I'll definitely try, I'd be a fool not to. So, then...! Onward toward whatever comes our way?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Though still appreciate the moment, yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You seemed to enjoy your first taste of Ambrosia, so I want you to have a bottle for yourself. For when the occasion warrants it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: A bottle for myself, when the occasion warrants it... wow, what? Oh, come on, Meli, this is really too much! I've nothing near this good to give you!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I beg to differ! You have given me much more. And I don't just mean the hammers you've been busy working on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've not done anything that scores of Shade admirers of yours wouldn't have done in a heartbeat, if they still had one.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Wait, you're a Shade admirer of mine?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
endLines.text: Oh, stop. And, give me that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How about we try this whole Ambrosia thing again, but this time, no pretending you are just another Shade. You're more than that. And more than a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Fine, but... don't you think this wasn't meant to be, hey? When I try to touch you it's barely a breeze, like I'm not even here...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You are here. I don't want to get hurt again either but each night I set out like this, I can't accomplish what I need to do without inviting the possibility... even the inevitability. Sometimes it's worth it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: My pain I can handle fine, who cares? Yours, I don't think I could live with... exist with, you know what I mean. But... argh! I can't believe I'm arguing with you, Meli, I'm happy more than anything right now! But I'm afraid...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: A fear we can face together.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How about one more Ambrosia, as a bit of an experiment, because a substance known for bringing warmth and pleasure seems somehow to bring you misery.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, excellent, I was just thinking I could use more misery and here we are. You do know why, don't you? We tried all this before, and it didn't work. I really wish it did, but hey.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm more experienced now. Sometimes when things don't work, you have to try again. Over, and over, and over, if that's what it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Meli, I agree with you... provided that the cost of trying isn't way too high! In your case, well... you've got another arm, there, sure. A couple legs. You're willing to put all those on the line, for me? To make me whole?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Look, I know I can't just permanently bring your body back. But this connection we have... that we've always had... I'm willing to go where it leads. I'd like to go, if you would too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I would, of course. I'd follow you into the sun, Meli. Or anywhere you'd rather be.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, this isn't more Ambrosia. It's called an Ectoplasmic Draught. Not as potent as the incantation I once tried, but this one works, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you saying, Meli, wait... this draught can make me whole? Or maybe partially, an arm or something, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It may be able to restore your sense of self as though you lived and breathed. But the effect won't last particularly long, perhaps a night at best. That was the compromise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're kidding, so... I could be me again... feel the cold wind and the spray of the sea and all that! For just one night. C-Can you make more?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Let's not get ahead of ourselves. These things can carry side effects if you don't take them slow. Swear that you will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I swear I'll take it slow using the Ectoplasmic Draught. I've ignored perfectly reasonable guidance to my peril at least once before and I don't intend to do it again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then please use this whenever you see fit, perhaps on some particularly special night. And treat it as one-of-a-kind.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
endLines.text: Thank you... thank you so much...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been working on something for quite a while here, and didn't want to say till it was ready, but I put it through more testing recently and think it's good to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh that's exciting. Your inventions must take quite a while to make ready and produce. What's this one all about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll see, but it makes just about everything better, kind of like, well, you. Don't have to try it if you don't want!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I never thought the goddess of the hunt would be here in the middle of all this. Figured they'd have her busy sniping targets on Olympus! She sings nice.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She does. Artemis is supposed to be keeping an eye on us, but she helps watch my back. I don't know how I even would have met the other gods if not for her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're cousins but you're more like sisters, hey? Must be reassuring to have someone like that looking out for you.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Was that a trace of jealousy?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
endLines.text: More like inadequacy. But it's gone!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: This place has changed so much since I took off. If you told me half this stuff was here, you'd have had a way easier time persuading me to come back.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wanted to see if you'd do it of your own accord. And also, surprise! You still remember the trick to locating the place?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hard to forget it once you know the way. Though, finding a good landing spot, I'm out of practice on that front. I nearly crashed into the cauldron!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: So you really do wake up each night back there, entirely refreshed? And with a newfound resolve to achieve your life's ambition, that sort of thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You make it sound so automatic. My physical recovery may be mostly second-nature by now, but my motivation certainly is not. You think I never get discouraged?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've never seen you discouraged. Though maybe you just hide it well. What'll you do if you succeed, hey? Pursue some other hopes and dreams?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: That's not something I've given any thought.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
endLines.text: I get it. First things first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: So you really do wake up each night back there, entirely refreshed? And with a newfound resolve to achieve your life's ambition, that sort of thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You make it sound so automatic. My physical recovery may be mostly second-nature by now, but my motivation certainly is not. You think I never get discouraged?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've never seen you discouraged. Though maybe you just hide it well.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: A gift I never knew I had!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you're up! I almost missed you, was about to fly on back to Thessaly. Hope you rested well. Sounds like you had quite an eventful night!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was productive, yes. Though if you're on assignment, I don't wish to keep you. We'll catch up some other time...! Or were you waiting here for me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: No! I mean, it's good to see you, yes, but... I'm not late. I can make up the time during my flight, you know, air currents and the like. So... well, back to it for the both of us then, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been flying for a while now! At first, it brought me back to the terror I felt as a kid. Those final moments... I wanted to put myself through them, again and again. Don't know why.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, you were young and you made a mistake. What's the use torturing yourself for it for all eternity? I thought you wanted to learn to fly to prove yourself to your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Meli. I'm never going to prove myself to him. But something happened as I kept trying to use my wings... I, I started to like it! Seeing from a different point of view.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You look so in your element up there! I always believed, if I could make you whole again, perhaps some night we'd learn to fly together. I still want to feel what it's like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've no idea know how much I wanted that too. But, it was something I had to do on my own. I'd offer to take you up with me now, but... yeah, Shades and goddesses don't mix.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been flying for a while now! At first, it brought me back to the terror I felt as a kid. Those final moments... I wanted to put myself through them, again and again. Don't know why.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, you were young and you made a mistake. What's the use torturing yourself for it for all eternity? I thought you wanted to learn to fly to prove yourself to your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Meli. I'm never going to prove myself to him. But something happened as I kept trying to use my wings... I, I started to like it! Seeing from a different point of view.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You look so in your element up there! I always believed, if I could make you whole again, perhaps some night we'd fly together...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've no idea know how much I wanted that too. But, it was something I had to do on my own. I'd offer to take you up with me now, but... yeah, Shades and goddesses don't mix.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Pleased to see you well again, Your Grace. Don't know about you, but last night was hard-fought for me! Those Chronos warship crews don't seem to tire out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, I thought I told you that I'm not Your Grace. I may be Princess of the Underworld by birth, but here I serve the Unseen, the same as you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know, it's just I don't want any special treatment here. Other Shades all seem to be polite with you. And, I like calling you Your Grace! As long as you don't mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tall fellow over there by that big tapestry type thing... isn't that Doom? Seems friendlier than I imagined, judging by the way the two of you were getting on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Moros is our guest, and that tapestry beside him holds the will of the Three Fates. His family's in a similar predicament to mine. Would you like to meet him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, no, we, erm... we've met before. Though I don't think he would remember me. It's for the best! I just didn't expect to see him again... let alone here. I'm glad he's all right!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tall fellow over there by that big tapestry type thing... isn't that Doom? Seems friendlier than I imagined, judging by the way the two of you were getting on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Moros is our guest, and that tapestry beside him holds the will of the Three Fates. Would you like to meet him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, no, we, erm... we've met before. Though I don't think he would remember me. It's for the best! I just didn't expect to see him again... let alone here. I'm glad he's all right!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I sometimes think of what it's like being a god. Ever wonder what your life would have been like if you were mortal, hey? You probably would have wound up in Elysium.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Among the greatest heroes of your kind? I'm not so certain about that. If I were born mortal, I suppose... I would have worked at something all my life. Attempted to excel beyond my means and those of anybody else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Meli, that's almost as depressing as my life, and I didn't get to choose. Most mortals work only to earn their keep... but it's the pleasures that we want, not the toil.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Truly? Yet here you are as a Shade, seeking to outdo your own master-artisan father at his craft, while battling against the Titan's legions. To earn your keep?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, I see your point, sorry. That was the mortal impulse to tell others how to live. But we rarely find our own sense of purpose, much less anyone else's. I wonder what you would have excelled at...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, do you know where Master Odysseus could have gone? I was supposed to report in tonight, though I haven't seen him in a little while...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think he had some personal matters that he needed to take care of. I'm uncertain when he'll return. Did your report contain anything urgent?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: No, it's just... I don't know. When he isn't there, I have this worry that he won't come back. He seems a wanderer at heart, and... isn't his own family someplace else?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Depends which family you mean! He isn't bound to the Crossroads any more than you. You may always return as long as you're welcome here, and I think he shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Like how you visited your family and still returned...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It never rains like this over the Rift. It's funny, growing up, I'd sometimes hear the sound of rain outside, but never got to feel it even once.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus would say you didn't miss much there, though he probably got rained on more than any mortal that ever lived. I quite like it, myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I do, too. Even got a taste of it thanks to your Ectoplasmic Draught. And even now, I still enjoy the look and sound of it... what it does to your hair and all that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My hair must look like dying branches, Icarus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's funny... we mortals go through life searching for that one other soul with whom we want to spend the rest of our nights; a love that lasts forever, but... we're really talking maybe thirty, forty years. Must be so different for the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To want for all of someone's love... it seems so selfish. Nemesis would surely disapprove. My understanding is we all have varying capacity for different types of love. In fact I think I've quite a bit for you now, Icarus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've quite a bit, wait you mean love, for me? Even though there must be others whom you fancy around here, in that primary-type-of-love sort of way. But what if... I have all this love for you, and no one else...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then I am very fortunate, though also I think you must have much more to give. And you've eternity to find all those who deserve it. I alone shall have to do for now.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
endLines.text: You're more than enough for me, Meli.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: That toolset there, is it really for me? Everything there looks so shiny. Where did we even get those from?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Consider it a gift from the Crossroads, Icarus. You shape the bounties of the Earth into wondrous things, so you might as well have the finest equipment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll have to try them out, thank you so much. I'm quite partial to the worn set I've been using for a while, but those are probably due for a break.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The storm that had been covering the top of Mount Olympus cleared up...! I figured I'd finally get to see Typhon, but no! What happened there?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The storm is gone because Typhon himself is gone. He seemed to be growing angrier each passing night. It was either him, or my family and their home.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're kidding me! He's gone as in, he's gone? Not hiding, dormant, nothing of the sort? Well! That's great! What are you doing here, though? You ought to be feasting with the rest of the Olympians right now!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Need to take care of a few things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nice seeing you again, Meli. I'm taking off, though I'll be sure to keep an eye out for you up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: If you decide to head up to the surface tonight, maybe I'll see you, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cheers to you all for welcoming me back, and always nice to see you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Time to get back into the fight, hey Meli? I'll go on ahead and see what I can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: At least I'm getting better at my landings and takeoffs here. Want to see?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I had to stop in for additional supplies, and possibly to say hello. And now I'm off!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Be on the lookout for my Hammers out there, hey? I hope they've been of help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know it's not the time to chat for long, though even just a little like this helps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just finished patching up these wings! And now to put them to the test again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's funny, I start getting restless whenever I'm not putting these wings to good use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wanted to say hello before I took off for the night. Take care then, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I knew you'd get there last night, and think you'll make it all the way this time too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe I'll see you out there on the Rift tonight, unless you're sticking to the Underworld?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Done my debrief with Master Odysseus so I'd best be heading back out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's see if I can dodge every last branch up there on my way out then, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Always good to see you here, and maybe I'll see you up top tonight too, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Seems like everything's been going all right over here. Especially by this fountain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Was hoping you'd stop by and say hello. See you again out there one of these nights?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Missed you out there on the Rift last night, though glad to see you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you did great last night, well done, Meli! Off for another go already, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Didn't think I'd feel this way, though it's good to be back, Meli! Oh, erm, Your Grace. This place feels different, think it's... warmer than it used to be or something, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You found your way back! You're welcome here and safe as ever, Icarus. I trust the wings made the return trip relatively swift and trouble-free?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, flying past the city of Ephyra sure beats having to trudge through. I still have to check in with Master Odysseus, though I'll try not be such a stranger around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
