dialogueLines.text: Been hearing Shades say something's changing on Olympus... that the Fates themselves decided that the gods will soon be ruling with a lighter touch? That sounds... different.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think soon there may be relative to gods not mortals, but... what I've heard is much the same. At the dawn of the new age, mortals are to choose their paths themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: An age where neither the gods nor the Fates are dictating each moment of our lives, or deaths. Hard to imagine. Who would we even blame if things went wrong then, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, when did you realize your father wasn't who you thought? Sounds like you grew up always wanting to live up to his idea of you, though at some point you stopped.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, it was probably when I found him after I died. He looked so disappointed; I genuinely thought he was upset I broke his precious wings. Kept working for him, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I see. Headmistress, she was like a mother to me, but... she always went out of her way to tell me she was not. I never really understood why.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You love her, I know that. And she put you through a lot, I know that too! If somebody is such a big part of your life for long enough, eventually it gets a bit complicated, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: The war with Chronos, it's really over, hey? The energy about this place is different, tension's gone, the other Shades practically bouncing up and down! You did it...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps, but... there's still more to be done. Preventative measures to ensure what happened never can occur again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Meli, can't you give yourself a break? Your task is finally complete! What are you going to do now, with the whole rest of your existence ahead of you?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I said there's still more to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I thought that once Chronos was out and the Underworld King and Queen were back, you'd have to join them in the House of Hades. Be the princess you were always meant to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd like to think I already am that. My father's House is truly something to behold, but this is home, not there. And I'll be seeing my parents and brother frequently enough, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, right! How was it meeting Zagreus? He live up to all the hushed tales of his prowess and all that?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: If I told you, I'd have to slay you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look what they did to you, those... augh! I'm sorry I didn't get here sooner, Meli.... Do you ever just turn back? I mean if you're this hurt already, why go on....

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not as hurt as you may think, and know my limits, Icarus. Though I appreciate the show of concern.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I just — It's hard to see you put yourself through this. Even if your wounds heal by tomorrow, what matters is right now. Anyway... I might have something to help keep you going.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Where have you been, Meli? Not seen you here or anywhere in far too many nights. Something's off, I don't know what but I think Chronos might be up to something new. His forces have been acting very strange...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: His forces have been acting very strange because Chronos abandoned his campaign! Icarus... our war is won! The House of Hades is restored! You'd not believe me if I told you how, but it's true!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos has given up, wait, what...? No! But we just fought more of his forces here! You're really serious...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When am I not? The Titan of Time is changed. We were able to get through to him finally, in the moment when his continued existence was on the line. I'm off to Olympus now, to make certain things are stable over there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I just, I can't believe it! Well, I mean, of course I can. Heh! You got him, finally! You did get him, didn't you? I, erm, I don't know what to say... have one of these?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Where have you been, Meli? Not seen you here or anywhere in far too many nights. Something's off, I don't know what but I think Chronos might be up to something new. His forces have been acting very strange...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: His forces have been acting very strange because Chronos abandoned his campaign! Icarus... our war is won! The House of Hades is restored! You'd not believe me if I told you how, but it's true!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos has given up, wait, what...? No! But we just fought more of his forces here! You're really serious...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When am I not? The Titan of Time is changed. We were able to get through to him finally, in the moment when his continued existence was on the line.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I just, I can't believe it! Well, I mean, of course I can. Heh! You got him, finally! You did get him, didn't you? I, erm, I don't know what to say... have one of these?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, last we spoke in the Crossroads, I... didn't mean to upset you, Meli. It's just... I didn't realize you'd have to keep fighting like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You asked me what I was going to do with the rest of my existence, and it set me off. I've not thought that far ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, it seems you don't need an answer anyway if you still have to keep heading up here. And probably down to the Underworld as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There's certainly a comfort in routine. But, I have to accept that this may be all I'm meant to do. All I'm proficient at.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't have to accept any such thing, Meli. All my father ever did was work. Felt it was his calling! But you're not like him. Anyway... the rest of existence can wait! Let's make this night a good one and go from there, hey?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: There really is more to be done then, hey? If the war with Chronos truly ended, it sure doesn't look that way from all these ships. The Harpies don't seem any friendlier, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Some must be zealots still believing that the Titan of Time shall rise again. But we're seeing to it that the threat of Chronos and Typhon can never recur. Just have to retrace my steps to Olympus as part of that process.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: So then... all right. Because if what you're saying is I get to keep on flying, and I get to keep on seeing you up here... that doesn't sound so bad to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, right! We thought the war ending would mean you'd have to put away your wings. But, my father's Underworld renovations seem like they'll be going on a long, long time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess I may need to head back to my designated spot eventually. But for now, I'd like to make the most of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, ever tried Ambrosia, Icarus? Because if not, you're about to. Given your inclination and availability, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: As a matter of fact, no, I've not tried Ambrosia before, and I've never had the inclination till you asked me just now. You sure it'd be all right...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The other Shades are watching us, Meli. They admire you so much, and probably would wish I was dead if I wasn't already.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh don't worry. If any of those Shades get too envious, the only attention they'll end up getting is from Nemesis. Why would they even care? I see them more than you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, it's just... as mortals many of us could get jealous. Especially when we'd see someone we cared for with somebody else. Hard to explain, but I think it all stems from our instinctively knowing how little time we have.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But you all have eternity now that you're here. Provided Chronos hasn't yet irreparably ruined everything. Though I suppose we gods can also have those types of feelings.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: Really? Come on, name one thing that's ever made you envious, Meli. You seem so comfortable the way you are, and with everything you have... I've never seen you want for anything that isn't yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then you haven't been paying attention. I'm envious of Headmistress, for one. I'll never be half the witch she is. Nor look nearly as elegant in hats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, I stand corrected! You're envious of the hat of the Witch of the Crossroads. Now can we try the drink already, hey? Could always spare some for the other Shades!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Wow. I have never had something like that in my entire life. Or death. The gods just regularly drink this stuff...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Got another bottle of Ambrosia we could split at the taverna if you're up for it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I would genuinely love to, though... we've had reports of a new fleet appearing in the Rift, and I'd best pay them a visit first.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Another taste of Ambrosia, with you? Right, race you there.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Long as you don't think it's too crowded at the taverna right now, sure.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: We celebrating something, hey? Though let's go even if we're not?

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...Hey think I know most of these other Shades by now. I like that one back there!

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...This stuff is really excellent, and somehow it tastes different every time.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: ...Tastes absolutely perfect. And they say we Shades can't taste a thing!

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Enjoyed the bit of company. And seems like everybody's in good spirits, more or less.

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Feeling lighter than a feather after that. I'm glad we went!

options.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
options.text: Nice seeing everyone back there, not to mention the drink.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I was just, eh, picking up a bit. Didn't mean to bother you, Goddess, erm... Hey! I like your wings! Mine aren't real. Though, I had an earlier pair made with real feathers, more like yours... but not nearly as good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eris_01
dialogueLines.text: How 'bout next time you see my stuff, you leave it be, all right there, baby bird? Now get out of my face and don't go tripping on your tongue on your way up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Also, I wanted to give thanks to you, Madam. For taking me in and letting me return; and for entrusting anything to me! Most Shades are not so fortunate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, do not grovel, Icarus, lest I cast you out. These are the Crossroads, not Elysium! A Shade's existence here may be comparatively difficult... and few remain for long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I appreciate having the work to do, and being a part of this. Although, sometimes just after taking off, I fear I won't be able to find my way back...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Discard that fear. Those with the need or who remain in our good graces always shall be welcome here and safe.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
endLines.text: I'm grateful. And, I'll be going! Cheers, Madam!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: As far as I can tell, the number of warships headed for Olympus has remained roughly the same night after night. Their mission doesn't seem to be affected in the slightest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Their aim is to cut off the waterways and spread the limited attention of Olympus thin. Continue to disrupt them as you can, my lad. Though if the Harpies flock too thick, fall back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: You got it, sir! Those ships are ill-equipped against attackers from the sky, and the Harpies haven't caught up with me yet. All right, I'm off!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Anyhow, my lad, you're doing fine. You can't always know where you're going, but you can have a sense for whether you're headed in the general direction you desire. And don't be in such a hurry to get there if you can help it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've not gone anywhere, though, sir. I thought if I could just learn how to fly, I'd finally be able to go wherever I pleased. But the farther away I got, the more I wanted to come back! What was the use? I'm still not sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The use was to find where you belonged. If you never left, you would have always wondered what else was out there for you. You returned with the wisdom of that experience.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Icarus_01
dialogueLines.text: So the more we wander, the wiser we get, hey? Except I'm speaking to the Great Tactician. I hardly think I've learned more flying about out there than I could have from you.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
endLines.text: I'll take the wings if you don't want them, lad.
